channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 68 homeless people removed from Southern California encampment
item.description: County officials removed 68 homeless residents from a South Los Angeles encampment during a recent operation. The effort, led by L.A. County’s Pathway Home program, was conducted with the help of local agencies and the county’s sheriff and fire departments from Nov. 20-22.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: The masked gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson remained elusive Friday, even as investigators learned more about his movements before and after the shocking crime. Investigators still don't know his name. They haven't found the gun.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Police find bag carried by gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, say he likely fled NYC on bus
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said Friday. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: What to know about Northern California's rare tsunami warning
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Northern California was under a rare and brief tsunami warning alert Thursday that tested local emergency notification systems after a 7.0 magnitude earthquake shook part of the state.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Man on trial in the killing of an Ole Miss student gave conflicting information, police say
item.description: The man on trial in the killing of University of Mississippi student Jimmie “Jay” Lee gave conflicting information to police about how well he and Lee knew each other.
item.dc:creator: Emily Wagster Pettus, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: MAP: Flu season is here, especially in these states
item.description: Flu activity spiked in states across the U.S. during the last week of November, according to a new report from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In California's 'earthquake country,' a 7.0 temblor prompts confusion and a tsunami warning
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Valerie Starkey was driving through Northern California to visit relatives when she suddenly felt shaking and feared her car had broken down, only to realize later that it was an earthquake so powerful that it triggered a tsunami warning for hundreds of miles of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: JetBlue nixing routes between multiple major cities, reducing several others
item.description: Passengers relying on JetBlue routes between certain American cities may need to make alternate travel plans in the near future.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Body of missing grandmother who fell into sinkhole recovered after 4-day search
item.description: The search for 64-year-old Elizabeth Pollard, who fell into a sinkhole in Pittsburgh, has concluded after the coroner's office confirmed with news outlets that her body had been found.
item.dc:creator: Amber Baker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: USDA requires bulk testing of raw milk supply for bird flu virus
item.description: The nation’s milk supply must be tested for the bird flu virus H5N1 under a new order announced Friday by the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) – here's why.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FEMA to cover Tampa couple's 'basement' after measurement mix-up
item.description: A Town N' Country couple's living room will be covered by flood insurance after all, following a call to Better Call Behnken.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Behnken, NewsNation

item.title: Appeals court upholds law ordering China-based ByteDance to sell TikTok or face U.S. ban
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday upheld a law requiring TikTok’s Chinese parent company to sell the popular app or face a U.S. ban.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KTLA 5

item.title: Millions will lose health coverage if ACA subsidies expire: CBO
item.description: If Affordable Care Act subsidies are allowed to expire at the end of 2025 millions of people will lose their insurance and premiums will jump, according to a Congressional Budget Office report.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KRON4

item.title: Teen killed, naked man arrested at California High School
item.description: A 24-year-old man, possibly naked and wielding an ax, was arrested in connection with the killing of a teenager in Newbury Park on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge upholds use of race in Naval Academy admissions, saying a diverse military is stronger
item.description: A federal judge has upheld the consideration of race in admissions at the U.S. Naval Academy, ruling that military cohesion and other national security factors mean the officer training school should not be subjected to the same standards as civilian universities.
item.dc:creator: Lea Skene, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Johnson woos his sharpest critics in preparation for Speaker vote
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is wooing his sharpest critics in the House GOP ahead of his official election for Speaker on the House floor, working to eliminate opposition and secure the strongest leadership mandate possible in a razor-thin majority. In the latest development, Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Crews recover the body of a woman from a Pennsylvania sinkhole after a 4-day search
item.description: Police say the remains of a woman who fell into a sinkhole have been recovered four days after she went missing while searching for her cat.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: These glasses let you 'see' conversations in real time
item.description: When I heard about AI glasses that let you "see" what people are saying, I was skeptical. But after trying them on, I was proven wrong. The Hearview AI Subtitle Glasses give you closed captions for the real world.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Wind-driven brushfire at Baltimore wood recycling yard closes expressway, light rail, schools
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Firefighters were battling a massive, wind-driven brushfire at a Baltimore wood recycling yard that closed a section of the expressway into the city’s downtown, the neighboring light rail line and nearby roads and schools on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Appeals court upholds TikTok ban law
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday upheld a law requiring TikTok’s Chinese parent company to sell the popular app or face a U.S. ban.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Company fined $172K after children employed to clean Iowa pork plant
item.description: They employed 11 children to "use corrosive cleaners to clean head splitters, jaw pullers, bandsaws, neck clippers and other equipment.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, KRON4

item.title: Missing teacher who was subject of Silver Alert found dead in back of vehicle
item.description: A Nashville teacher who last seen nearly three weeks ago has been found dead, authorities announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Patton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New clues emerge as investigators hunt for the gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New clues emerged Thursday in the hunt for the masked gunman who stalked and killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, including possible leads about his travel before the shooting and a message scrawled on ammunition found at the crime scene.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 7.0 earthquake off Northern California prompts brief tsunami warning
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A 7.0 magnitude earthquake shook a large area of Northern California on Thursday, knocking items off grocery store shelves, sending children scrambling under desks and prompting a brief tsunami warning for 5.3 million people along the U.S. West Coast.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Splash pads associated with waterborne disease outbreaks: CDC
item.description: "Children are at increased risk for exposure to pathogens in contaminated splash pad water," the CDC said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Yes, Chipotle is raising menu prices again: Here's why
item.description: The cost of your burrito bowl is going up. Chipotle has raised menu prices for the first time in over a year.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KRON4

item.title: Fake bomb threat targets homes of insurance executive killed in NYC: Police
item.description: The threat was sent in an email to multiple people, who in turn alerted law enforcement, according to incident reports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Secret Service director touts changes as Congress presses him on Trump assassination attempt
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The acting director of the Secret Service said Thursday that the agency is “reorganizing and reimagining” its culture and how it operates following an assassination attempt against Donald Trump on the campaign trail.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US judge rejects Boeing's plea deal in a conspiracy case stemming from fatal plane crashes
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday rejected a deal that would have let Boeing plead guilty to a felony conspiracy charge and pay a fine for misleading U.S. regulators about the 737 Max jetliner before two of the planes crashed, killing 346 people. U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KOENIG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nearly a dozen cars stolen with kids left inside over Thanksgiving, car safety group warns
item.description: A child car safety group is warning parents not to leave children unattended in their vehicles this holiday season after nearly a dozen cars were stolen over Thanksgiving with kids left inside.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Link to adult website on Mattel's 'Wicked' dolls at center of proposed class action lawsuit
item.description: A mother claims she and her daughter visited the link on the box, only to be "horrified" by what they saw, according to the lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: QVC recalls 1.1 million oven gloves after more than 90 burn injury reports
item.description: QVC is recalling more than 1 million pairs of oven gloves after dozens of people were burned while using them, the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission has announced.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: You Are Right to Hate DC
item.description: Worth a click: Transgender rights activists went into the women’s bathroom at the Capitol in an act of “c**** civil disobedience” — their words, not mine. Watch the video here. Deck Chairs on the Titanic DOGE in the house: Elon Musk got a hero’s welcome on Capitol Hill today.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: 7.0-magnitude Humboldt County quake reportedly felt 350 miles away
item.description: The strong earthquake that jolted Northern California Thursday morning was felt far from its Humboldt County epicenter.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Secret Service leader, GOP rep get into shouting match over 9/11 questioning
item.description: Acting Secret Service Director Ronald Rowe and Rep. Pat Fallon (R-Texas) engaged in a shouting match that persisted over gavel banging, as Rowe accused the lawmaker of politicizing the Sept. 11 attack.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Teen found guilty of beating disabled DC man Reggie Brown to death sentenced to 7 years
item.description: One of the younger victims convicted of beating Washington, D.C., disabled resident Reggie Brown will be in prison until she is 21 years old following her sentencing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Frontier Airlines to roll out first-class seating, other perks in 2025
item.description: Frontier Airlines will offer first-class seating for the first time in its history starting in late 2025, the company announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, KTLA 5
